name: Okta API
what_it_does: Enterprise-grade identity platform providing SSO, MFA, lifecycle management, and API access management for workforce and customer identity use cases.
use_cases: Enterprise workforce identity - managing employee login and access
Customer identity (CIAM) for large-scale consumer apps
Automated user provisioning/deprovisioning via SCIM or API
API access management and token validation
Audit log querying for compliance and security investigations
not_for: Small teams or startups (expensive and complex for simple use cases)
Teams wanting quick dev-friendly setup (steep learning curve)
best_when: Your organization already uses Okta for workforce identity or you're building for large enterprises that require Okta integration.
avoid_when: You need fast setup, are a startup, or don't have enterprise identity requirements.

auth.notes: SSWS API key for quick access; OAuth2 with fine-grained scopes for production. SSWS tokens don't expire which is agent-friendly but a security risk. OAuth2 recommended for production agents.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free developer org available; production requires paid plan
pricing.paid_tiers: Workforce Identity from $2/user/month, Customer Identity custom pricing
pricing.notes: Enterprise pricing. Developer orgs available for free but production use requires contract. Pricing varies significantly by features and volume.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by endpoint: 600-1200 requests/minute for most user APIs

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Structured error responses with errorCode, errorSummary, and errorCauses arrays. Well-documented but verbose.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: User/group operations are idempotent on unique identifiers. No explicit idempotency keys for API calls.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limits are per-org not per-API-key - multiple agents sharing an org hit combined limits
SSWS tokens never expire by default - must be manually rotated (security risk for agents)
Event Hooks (webhooks) require manual verification challenge flow before activation
System Log API has a delay of up to 1-2 minutes - not suitable for real-time event processing
User states are a complex lifecycle (staged, provisioned, active, locked, deprovisioned) - agents must handle all states
